title: Looking to migrate from Trello to Monday.com without losing data
excerpt:
I would like to move my data from Trello to Monday.com 
I’ve checked this link and there is so much data left behind, making the boards unusable. Is there any tool that makes import / export from Trello to Monday easier,…

posts.author: Jamie-Carten (Jamie Carten)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to move my data from Trello to Monday.com
I’ve checked this link and there is so much data left behind, making the boards unusable. Is there any tool that makes import / export from Trello to Monday easier, bringing comments, assignees, Subitems (checklists) and attachments over>
Thanks for reading.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Jamie-Carten

For a smooth transition of your data from Trello to Monday.com without losing any critical elements like comments, assignees, checklists, and attachments,

we can leverage a few automation tools to ensure everything moves seamlessly. The approach would involve setting up a system using Make.com or Zapier, where we can automate the entire data migration process to capture all your board details, including attachments, subitems, and comments, and import them directly into Monday.com.

We would create a custom automation that works around Trello’s API and Monday’s, ensuring all your boards, lists, tasks, and comments are mapped correctly. The process involves:

Mapping Trello data structure to Monday’s structure.
Transferring all attachments and comments to their corresponding fields.
Handling assignees and checklist items to maintain task integrity.

Let’s Schedule the call to discuss

posts.author: Jonathan-Miller (Jonathan Miller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Jamie,
You can use a 3rd party tool like Altosio.
Altosio migrates your Trello data to Monday and preserves the comments, attachments and subtasks (checklists).

posts.author: Jamie-Carten (Jamie Carten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much @Jonathan-Miller
I used Altosio and it everything worked perfectly!
